narrativeOwner: Prof. Ian Reid, Acting Director of Research Administration & Division Deans

assessment.headline: Research output is strong and translating into external traction. BLS's first Nature-family paper is already in clinical pilot at Cleveland Clinic, and the external funding portfolio has grown by AED 8M (+10%) this month with new grants from Google and Microsoft. But the partnership infrastructure hasn't kept pace — a third of MOUs are dormant and formal ownership is pending Carlos's arrival in April.
whereWeStand: Patent filings accelerated this month — 3 new applications, including the first with a student co-inventor.
The Google grant has generated significant media coverage and positions MBZUAI in the responsible AI conversation alongside major industry labs.
Computing and Mathematical Sciences has gone three months without a Tier 1 publication — unusual given the division's size.
8 of 22 signed MOUs have no active engagement. The dormant MIT CSAIL partnership may be reactivated through a faculty visit scheduled for April.
whatsNext: Alsakhar full proposals due mid-April with external reviews to follow. **Deadline: April 15 · Owner: Director of Research Admin**
Head of Research Ethics and Compliance expected to be hired by April — currently a gap in the organizational structure. **Deadline: April 30 · Owner: VP Faculty Excellence & HR**
Research admin workflows blocked on ERP system upgrade, not scheduled until April. **Deadline: April · Owner: IT & Research Admin**

metrics.label: Tier 1 publications (YTD)
metrics.trendLabel: up from 31 same period last year

publications.tier1Definition: Tier 1: Nature, Science, PNAS, top-4 AI conferences (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR) and top language-AI venues (ACL, EMNLP).

flagshipPublications.title: Multi-Modal Foundation Models for Precision Oncology
flagshipPublications.abstract: We present a multi-modal foundation model that integrates genomic, proteomic, and clinical imaging data for precision oncology treatment selection. Evaluated on 12,000 patient records across 8 cancer types, the model achieves state-of-the-art treatment response prediction, outperforming single-modality approaches by 23%. The model is being deployed in a pilot program with Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi.
flagshipPublications.significance: First Nature-family publication from Biological and Life Sciences. Validates the cross-disciplinary AI-for-science strategy. Clinical deployment pilot with Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi adds real-world impact narrative for government and donor conversations.

flagshipPublications.title: Scaling Laws for Arabic Language Model Pre-Training
flagshipPublications.abstract: We establish scaling laws for Arabic-centric language model pre-training, demonstrating that Arabic models require different compute-optimal ratios than English-centric models due to morphological complexity. Findings directly inform the JAIS model training pipeline and have reduced pre-training costs by an estimated 18%.
flagshipPublications.significance: Directly relevant to JAIS — provides scientific foundation for MBZUAI's flagship model development. Co-authored by newly signed faculty (Petrova) and an Emirati MSc student.

flagshipPublications.title: Provably Efficient Reinforcement Learning Under Non-Stationary Dynamics
flagshipPublications.abstract: We provide the first provably efficient algorithm for reinforcement learning in environments with non-stationary transition dynamics, achieving near-optimal regret bounds. The theoretical framework has applications in financial trading and autonomous systems.
flagshipPublications.significance: Natural and Physical Sciences' strongest publication this year. Prof. Moulines is leading from the front — important signal given the division's smaller size.

flagshipPublications.title: Real-Time 3D Scene Reconstruction for Autonomous Navigation in Unstructured Environments
flagshipPublications.abstract: We present a real-time neural scene reconstruction system that operates in unstructured outdoor environments with dynamic obstacles. The system achieves 30fps reconstruction on edge hardware, making it deployable on autonomous vehicles and delivery robots. Developed in collaboration with the Agentic Robotics Lab.
flagshipPublications.significance: Joint work with newly signed faculty (Tanaka, starting Sep 2026) and the Agentic Robotics Lab. Demonstrates Engineering and Applied Sciences' robotics capability — relevant to the lab's strategic roadmap.

flagshipPatents.title: Method for Privacy-Preserving Federated Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
flagshipPatents.description: A novel method for fine-tuning large language models across distributed datasets without exposing raw data. Combines differential privacy with a new gradient compression technique that reduces communication overhead by 85% while maintaining model accuracy within 2% of centralized training.
flagshipPatents.significance: IP from incoming faculty (Al-Rashidi) — demonstrates research contribution before formal start date. Potential licensing value for enterprise LLM deployment.

flagshipPatents.title: AI-Driven Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Prediction System
flagshipPatents.description: A system for predicting protein-ligand binding affinity using a graph neural network architecture trained on structural and sequence data. Achieves 15% improvement over existing methods on standard benchmarks. Developed in collaboration with a pharmaceutical partner.
flagshipPatents.significance: Companion IP to the Nature Machine Intelligence publication. Student co-inventor (Amira Nasser) — signals student involvement in IP creation. Pharmaceutical partner interest suggests commercialization potential.

flagshipGrants.title: Google Research Grant — Arabic AI Safety and Alignment
flagshipGrants.description: Research grant focused on developing safety and alignment techniques specifically for Arabic language models, including JAIS. Covers bias detection, cultural sensitivity evaluation, and red-teaming methodologies for Arabic-centric AI systems.
flagshipGrants.significance: The $1M announcement generated significant media and social engagement. Validates Google's recognition of MBZUAI's Arabic language AI leadership. Directly supports JAIS safety layer development.

flagshipGrants.title: ASPIRE Grant — Autonomous Systems for Extreme Environments
flagshipGrants.division: Engineering and Applied Sciences / Research Infrastructure
flagshipGrants.description: Development of autonomous perception and navigation systems for extreme environments including desert, underwater, and high-temperature industrial settings. Collaboration with the Agentic Robotics Lab and ADNOC for industrial testing.
flagshipGrants.significance: Largest ASPIRE grant to MBZUAI to date. Cross-divisional collaboration (Engineering and Applied Sciences + Research Infrastructure). ADNOC industrial testing component adds government and industry engagement narrative.

flagshipGrants.title: AI-Driven Precision Agriculture for Arid Climates
flagshipGrants.funder: Abu Dhabi Agriculture and Food Safety Authority
flagshipGrants.description: Developing AI models for crop yield prediction and water optimization in arid climates. Pilot deployment planned at Al Ain research farms.
flagshipGrants.significance: Extends BLS into applied AI-for-agriculture, a strategic priority for UAE food security. Government-funded — adds to the Abu Dhabi institutional relationship portfolio.

flagshipGrants.title: Multilingual Foundation Model Evaluation Framework
flagshipGrants.description: Building a comprehensive evaluation framework for multilingual foundation models, with a focus on Arabic, Hindi, and other underrepresented languages. Outputs will be open-sourced.
flagshipGrants.significance: Strengthens the Microsoft relationship beyond cloud infrastructure. Open-source outputs position MBZUAI as a standard-setting institution for multilingual AI evaluation.

flagshipGrants.title: Quantum-Classical Hybrid Optimization for Drug Discovery
flagshipGrants.description: Exploring quantum-classical hybrid algorithms for molecular optimization in drug discovery. Cross-divisional project between NPS and BLS.
flagshipGrants.significance: Mubadala-funded — deepens the investment partner relationship. Cross-divisional collaboration signals institutional research maturity.

enrichedPartners.name: Google
enrichedPartners.focusArea: Arabic AI, safety research, cloud infrastructure
enrichedPartners.keyContactsMBZUAI: Prof. Monojit Choudhury, Prof. Thamar Solorio

activityHistory.note: $1M Arabic AI Safety grant announced. Media coverage generated across Gulf News, The National, and international tech outlets.
Renewed cloud infrastructure agreement for JAIS training compute.
Joint workshop on Arabic LLM benchmarking held at MBZUAI campus.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: Joint paper submission to EMNLP 2026 on Arabic AI safety benchmarks. Google VP visit to campus planned for Q2 2026.
enrichedPartners.notes: Strategic relationship. Google sees MBZUAI as primary Arabic AI research partner. Relationship managed by Prof. Choudhury.

enrichedPartners.name: TII (Technology Innovation Institute)
enrichedPartners.focusArea: LLM development, JAIS collaboration, autonomous systems
enrichedPartners.keyContactsMBZUAI: Prof. Eric Xing, Prof. Monojit Choudhury

activityHistory.note: 2 MBZUAI graduates joined TII AI Cross-Center Unit in senior researcher roles.
JAIS model co-development meeting — agreed on next pre-training run parameters.
Joint NDA signed for Falcon-JAIS interoperability research.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: Next JAIS co-development sprint scheduled for April 2026.
enrichedPartners.notes: Core institutional relationship. TII is the primary JAIS development partner and top graduate employer.

enrichedPartners.name: KAUST
enrichedPartners.focusArea: Student exchange, joint research, protein structure prediction

activityHistory.note: PhD student Chen Wei selected for KAUST Visiting Researcher program (3-month placement on protein structure prediction).
Annual joint research review meeting held at KAUST. 3 joint papers published in 2025.
Student exchange agreement renewed for 2025-26 academic year.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: Joint workshop on AI for drug discovery planned for May 2026.
enrichedPartners.notes: Strong academic partnership. KAUST is also a significant feeder for graduate applications.

enrichedPartners.name: Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi
enrichedPartners.focusArea: AI for precision medicine, clinical pilot deployments

activityHistory.note: Clinical pilot deployment initiated for multi-modal precision oncology model (published in Nature Machine Intelligence).
Data sharing agreement signed for de-identified patient records (12,000 records across 8 cancer types).
MOU signed for AI in healthcare collaboration.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: Pilot results review scheduled for June 2026. Potential expansion to cardiovascular AI diagnostics.
enrichedPartners.notes: High-impact healthcare partnership. The Nature MI publication + clinical pilot creates a strong narrative for government and donor audiences.

enrichedPartners.name: ADNOC
enrichedPartners.focusArea: Industrial AI, autonomous systems, predictive maintenance

activityHistory.note: 3rd cohort of AI Leadership Program (Academy) delivered for 28 ADNOC senior managers.
ASPIRE autonomous systems grant (AED 3.2M) signed with ADNOC as industrial testing partner.
Predictive maintenance pilot completed at ADNOC Ruwais facility.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: 4th AI Leadership Program cohort planned for Q3 2026. Autonomous systems field testing at ADNOC sites beginning Q2.
enrichedPartners.notes: Multi-faceted relationship spanning research (ASPIRE grant), executive education (Academy), and industrial deployment. ADNOC is a flagship UAE industry partner.

enrichedPartners.name: MIT CSAIL
enrichedPartners.focusArea: Robotics — no activity since MOU signing

activityHistory.note: MOU signed during MIT delegation visit. Focus on robotics and autonomous systems research collaboration.
Follow-up email sent to CSAIL team. No response received.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: MIT CSAIL faculty visit planned for April 10, 2026 — opportunity to reactivate.
enrichedPartners.notes: Dormant since signing. The upcoming April visit is a critical opportunity to reactivate this partnership.

enrichedPartners.name: University of Toronto
enrichedPartners.keyContactsMBZUAI: Prof. Thamar Solorio, Dr. Maria Petrova

activityHistory.note: Dr. Maria Petrova (signed Jan 2026) formally transitions from UofT. Joint supervision of 2 PhD students continues.
Faculty exchange agreement signed. One UofT postdoc spent fall semester at MBZUAI.

enrichedPartners.upcoming: Joint multilingual NLP workshop being planned for fall 2026.
enrichedPartners.notes: Relationship deepened through Petrova hire. Natural collaboration bridge for multilingual NLP research.

enrichedPartners.name: IIT Bombay

activityHistory.note: IIT Bombay leadership delegation visited MBZUAI (4 delegates). Draft MOU to be circulated by end of March.

enrichedPartners.notes: IIT Bombay is the single largest feeder university for MBZUAI graduate applications (380 applications this cycle). Formalizing the relationship strengthens a critical pipeline.

partnerships.note: Partnership data is based on a preliminary inventory compiled by the Strategy team. A centralized partnership registry is being established. Current data may be incomplete.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: Trustworthy & Ethical ML Workshop
upcomingResearchEvents.location: Executive Theater, Knowledge Center, MBZUAI, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Two-day workshop on securing agentic and frontier AI systems. Core topics include security and privacy for advanced AI, interpretability and control, scalable oversight, dangerous capability evaluations, and AI governance and policy.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: AI for Earth Observation & Climate Symposium
upcomingResearchEvents.location: MBZUAI, Masdar City, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Co-organized with ADIA Lab. Invitation-only symposium on AI-driven approaches for Earth observation, climate science, and geospatial analysis. Topics span foundation models for Earth observation, multimodal and spatiotemporal learning, uncertainty-aware and physics-informed AI, climate and weather modeling, extreme event analysis, and scalable systems for global environmental monitoring.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: Biological and Life Sciences Division Symposium
upcomingResearchEvents.location: MBZUAI, Masdar City, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Annual divisional symposium showcasing BLS research across computational biology, genomics, and AI for life sciences.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: Time, Space and Shape
upcomingResearchEvents.location: MBZUAI, Masdar City, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Focused workshop bringing together ~30 researchers working on time series, spatio-temporal processes, and functional/shape data analysis. Topics include time series modeling in high-dimensional settings, change-point and anomaly detection, spatio-temporal statistics, functional data analysis, shape analysis, scalable computation for large datasets, and uncertainty quantification. Graduate students and early career researchers encouraged.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: Agentic AI Security Symposium: From Autonomy to Assurance
upcomingResearchEvents.location: MBZUAI, Masdar City, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Symposium on LLM/agent security, privacy, and trustworthy AI. Features keynotes, invited talks, and panel on securing tool-use and computer-use agents. Core topics include security for agentic systems (tool APIs, browsing, computer-use, RAG/memory), prompt/indirect injection, red-teaming and evaluations, secure-by-design agent architectures, interpretability and scalable oversight, and deployment governance.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: Stats Foundations of AI
upcomingResearchEvents.location: MBZUAI, Masdar City, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Workshop at the intersection of statistics and AI. Topics include statistics for trustworthy AI (reliability, robustness, interpretability, calibration), uncertainty quantification, mathematics of modern deep learning architectures including transformers, pretraining and post-training/alignment, diffusion models, and formal characterizations of chain-of-thought reasoning and emergent behaviors.

upcomingResearchEvents.name: Exploring the Human and Planetary Microbiome at Scale
upcomingResearchEvents.location: MBZUAI, Masdar City, Abu Dhabi, UAE
upcomingResearchEvents.description: Symposium on microbial life across Earth and beyond. Spans experimental to computational biology, integrating field studies, high-throughput sequencing, bioinformatics, and AI-driven analytics. Themes include the planetary virome, microbiomes in food systems and nutrition, large-scale metagenomics, structural biology, and machine learning for discovery of genes, biomarkers, and host-virus interactions.
